title: Clinical Decision Support Response Time
spl:
index=healthcare sourcetype="cds:query"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(query_time_ms) as avg_ms, perc95(query_time_ms) as p95_ms, count by rule_id, _time
| where p95_ms > 500
| table _time, rule_id, avg_ms, p95_ms, count
description: CDS alerts must fire in real-time during clinical workflows. Excessive latency causes clinicians to bypass alerts, reducing patient safety benefit.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Clinical Decision Support Response Time to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Instrument CDS engine with response time logging. Track by rule type and clinical context. Optimize slow rules that impact order entry workflows. Alert when latency exceeds clinical usability thresholds. **Domain context:** CDS Hooks / SMART targets sub-second response for interruptive alerts in some workflows; batch analytics rules differ. **Splunk:** Separate interruptive vs background CDS if `rule_id` encodes workflow type.
visualization: Line chart (response time by rule), Heatmap (rule √Ч time), Table (slowest rules).
knownFalsePositives: CDS delay spikes on knowledge-base refreshes, new order-set rollouts, heavy cohort analytics jobs, or content freezes during a clinical build freeze.

references.title: Splunk Lantern вАФ use case library

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: CDS engine logs via HEC.
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="cds:query"` fields `rule_id`, `query_time_ms`, `result`, `triggered_alert`.
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
Instrument CDS engine with response time logging. Track by rule type and clinical context. Optimize slow rules that impact order entry workflows. Alert when latency exceeds clinical usability thresholds. **Domain context:** CDS Hooks / SMART targets sub-second response for interruptive alerts in some workflows; batch analytics rules differ. **Splunk:** Separate interruptive vs background CDS if `rule_id` encodes workflow type.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=healthcare sourcetype="cds:query"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(query_time_ms) as avg_ms, perc95(query_time_ms) as p95_ms, count by rule_id, _time
| where p95_ms > 500
| table _time, rule_id, avg_ms, p95_ms, count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Clinical Decision Support Response Time** вАФ CDS alerts must fire in real-time during clinical workflows. Excessive latency causes clinicians to bypass alerts, reducing patient safety benefit.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="cds:query"` fields `rule_id`, `query_time_ms`, `result`, `triggered_alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): CDS engine logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: healthcare; **sourcetype**: cds:query. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Scopes the data: index=healthcare, sourcetype="cds:query". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
вАҐ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rule_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
вАҐ Filters the current rows with `where p95_ms > 500` вАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
вАҐ Pipeline stage (see **Clinical Decision Support Response Time**): table _time, rule_id, avg_ms, p95_ms, count


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Compare Splunk results with the EHR, LIS, pharmacy, or device vendor view and spot-check fields against the Epic/Cerner (or equivalent) audit export for the same user, patient context, or device and time range.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (response time by rule), Heatmap (rule √Ч time), Table (slowest rules).
grandmaExplanation: We watch clinical decision support response times. We help you catch delays before alerts arrive too late to matter.
